message:
Last winter, a nasty ice storm knocked out power for two days. My kids were *devastated* — no screens, no Wi-Fi. I genuinely braced myself.

Then something funny happened. We pulled out an old deck of cards, some colored pencils, and a stack of library books. Suddenly they weren't bored; they were *engaged*. We played games by flashlight, drew pictures, told stories. My oldest even asked if we could do it again.

Now I realize: those low-tech things aren't just backup entertainment — they're actually *better* than I remembered. And when you think about severe weather prep, having a few games, some art supplies, or books stashed away? That's not just practical; it takes the edge off an already stressful situation.

Storms still aren't fun, but having that stuff on hand made ours feel a lot less chaotic.

Do you have a favorite low-tech activity your family does? Would love to hear what keeps yours sane during power outages. 🔦
